title: Send Friend Request
description: The system shall send a friend request to another user and notify the recipient.
rationale: To facilitate connections between users, allowing them to initiate social interactions within the platform.
business_value: Enhances user engagement by providing a means for users to connect with others, thereby increasing community interaction and satisfaction.

source.description: As a user, I need to send friend requests to other users and accept or reject incoming requests so that I can connect with others and manage my network.

acceptance_criteria: The recipient receives a notification about the pending friend request.
When accepted, the sender and receiver are both marked as friends, and each gets a notification of this change.
When rejected, the sender is notified that their request has been rejected.
preconditions: The user must be logged in to the system.
There should be another user available to send a friend request to.
postconditions: A new pending friend request is added to the recipient's list.
If accepted, a mutual friendship status is established between the sender and receiver.
If rejected, the request disappears from both users' lists of pending requests.
security_requirements: User identities must be verified before allowing friend requests to be sent or received.
All communications related to friend requests must be encrypted.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Friend request processing should occur within a reasonable time frame, typically less than 5 seconds.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time during business hours.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should handle an increasing number of concurrent users and friend requests without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages for failed requests should be clear and helpful, guiding the user on how to proceed.

edge_cases: What happens if the recipient is offline?
How does the system handle duplicate friend requests?
What should happen if a user tries to send a request to themselves?
What if there are network issues when sending or receiving notifications?
dependencies: Database for storing friendship statuses and requests

interfaces.name: Friendship API
interfaces.description: Interface for sending, accepting, and rejecting friend requests.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: Stores user information and friendship statuses.

interfaces.name: Friend Request Notification
interfaces.description: Displays notifications to users about incoming friend requests.

test_cases.description: User sends a friend request to another user who is online.
test_cases.expected_result: The recipient receives a notification of the pending request.

test_cases.description: User tries to send a friend request to themselves.
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the request and notifies the user with an error message.

test_cases.description: User accepts a pending friend request from another user.
test_cases.expected_result: Both users are marked as friends, and each receives a notification of this change.

test_cases.description: User rejects a pending friend request from another user.
test_cases.expected_result: The request is removed, and the sender is notified that their request has been rejected.
